title: [Feature Request] Saner workaround for blocked "app lock screen" whenever device is screen mirroring to pc
summary: Issue: Opening the whole app to enable/disable the service is too tedious currently. 1. Device is connected to pc and automatically screen mirrored via [scrcpy](https://scrcpy.org/). 2. Opened some app behind App Lock. 3. App lock screen launches, but the screen is blocked, so cannot touch type / keyboard type the password. Solutions: 1. Allow typing pin via connected devices + enter as confirmation. 2. Add top dropdown tray icon to enable/disable app lock service entirely. 3. Add option in app lock settings, to permanently enable/disable screen blocking while screen sharing/mirroring/capturing/etc.
original_title: [Feature Request] Saner workaround for blocked "app lock screen" whenever device is screen mirroring to pc

title: Using a custom agent in this app is frustrating.
summary: Using a custom agent in this app is frustrating. It keeps switching, but when I try to figure out how to start a session using my preferred agent, I seem to go in circles. The final suggested fix is for me to have to remember to ask copilot to use specific things, when all I want is for the dropdowns on the prompt to actually work. Here is the conversation: Use a **new explicit session** with `kickoff.agent: "asgard:odin"` (plus a `kickoff.prompt`) to lock it in more consistently. ## You said: But I am using this app, and I do not see anyplace to put this json for session creation. ## Copilot said: You’re right—**in this app UI there usually isn’t a raw JSON input box** for session creation. That payload is for the underlying `create_session` API/tool call. In the app, you either: 1. Use the **Create/New Session** form fields (project, local/cloud, agent, mode), or 2. Have me execute it directly as a tool call from chat. So you don’t paste that JSON into a prompt window; it maps to UI controls or a tool invocation. ## You said: Y'all know this is extremely not user friendly, right? I am in the app and you are telling me to use json that I can't use, and then I ask if the options on the prompt do the same thing and the answer seems to be not really. ## Copilot said: You’re absolutely right — **that is frustrating UX**, and your read is correct. In this app, the prompt-window options are **soft preferences** and can be overridden by workflow/session defaults, while explicit session creation is a **hard setup action** behind the scenes. So yes, they don’t behave the same, and that mismatch is confusing. The practical fix here is: have me do it directly in-chat with a command-style request (no JSON needed), e.g. **“Create a new local session for this repo using asgard:odin, interactive mode, with kickoff prompt: …”** That uses explicit session creation and avoids the agent-switching behavior you’re seeing. --- | Field | Value | | --- | --- | | App version
original_title: Using a custom agent in this app is frustrating.
